scope: dispatchers, counters, webhooks, double-submit, twilio idempotency, shadow-diff collisions

findings.title: Generic shadow-diff doc-id collides under burst (uid__Date.now())
findings.vector: 17 dispatchers (vidya, quiz, lesson-plan, exam-paper, instant-answer, rubric, teacher-training, video-storyteller, virtual-field-trip, visual-aid, voice-to-text, worksheet, parent-message, avatar-generator, assessment-scanner, assignment-assessor, community-persona-message) write here. With Q4C canary-observation now firing BOTH a sidecar primary AND a parallel Genkit shadow per request from the SAME uid, two shadow_diff writes for one logical request frequently land in the same millisecond → second write silently overwrites first via .set().
repro_preview_steps: Hit /api/ai/quiz with same auth header 10× via Promise.all from a script
findings.impact: Parity-rollup loses samples; Q4C promotion gate evaluates on a lossy denominator; regressions slip past canary→full because their evidence row was overwritten.
findings.fix: Append randomUUID().slice(0,8) suffix: `${uid}__${Date.now()}__${randomUUID().slice(0,8)}`. Alternative: collection.add() for Firestore auto-id.
findings.blast_radius: all 17 sidecar dispatchers in shadow + canary + full modes

findings.title: toggleLike TOCTOU inflates likesCount on concurrent likes
findings.race: Read-then-write. N concurrent likes from same UID → 1 like-doc (set idempotent) but likesCount inflated by N.
findings.comment_misleading: Comment at community.ts:92 claims 'replaced read-modify-write with FieldValue.increment so two concurrent toggles can't ... lose a count' — but the toggle DIRECTION (like vs unlike) is decided by the pre-read, which is not atomic. The fix solved only HALF the problem.
findings.trigger: Double-tap on slow network; React strict-mode dev fires server actions twice; intentional spam click
findings.fix: Wrap entire toggle inside db.runTransaction so read+set+increment is serialised. Alternatively use .create()/.delete() and catch ALREADY_EXISTS as no-op.

findings.title: saveResource TOCTOU inflates stats.saves
findings.race: Same pattern as F5-002 but for community library saves. 1 save-doc, +N counter on N concurrent saves.
findings.user_library_safe: Personal-library copy uses deterministic contentId, so no duplicate appears in the saver's library. Only counter is wrong.
findings.fix: runTransaction around read saveRef + set + increment.

findings.title: Profile double-submit duplicates fan-out notifications
findings.race: Two concurrent first-save requests both observe existingProfile=null → both fan out new-teacher notifications to every nearby teacher.
findings.blast_radius: FCM cost doubles; receiving teachers see N notifications for one join; trust erosion in the directory.
findings.fix: Before firing fanout, .create() a guard doc welcome_fanout_sent/{uid}. ALREADY_EXISTS → skip.

findings.title: Razorpay 'failed' → retry CAS window allows concurrent handlers
findings.race: Two retries of a previously-failed event both read status='failed' and both flip to 'processing' without atomic CAS. Both handlers run. Today's payloads are idempotent at the value level so no double-charge. Risk is latent.
findings.fix: Wrap failed→processing flip in db.runTransaction with tx.get() pre-check; throw sentinel on CAS-mismatch and return already_processed.

findings.title: Twilio twiml retry duplicates transcript + collides shadow-diff
findings.race: Read-modify-write of (turnCount, transcript) with no per-turn idempotency lock. When Twilio retries a slow webhook, two concurrent invocations both compute the SAME turnNumber, both call the dispatcher, both push (parent, agent) to transcript, and last write wins.
effects: transcript divergence — Firestore stores reply from whichever request finished last; parent heard the FIRST
shadow-diff collision on ${callSid}__${turnNumber:04d} — one row lost
post-call summary shown to teacher uses the wrong agent branch
findings.fix: Claim per-turn lock with .create('outreach/{id}/turn_locks/{newTurnCount}') at entry; ALREADY_EXISTS → return cached TwiML stored on the lock doc.

non_findings_verified_safe.result: doc-id is `${callSid}__${turnNumber:04d}` — deterministic by design. No same-millisecond burst risk. Collides only on twiml RETRY (F5-006).
Properly wrapped in db.runTransaction with t.get(memberRef) gating the count update. Re-entry safe.
Inside the same runTransaction as the message write — safe.
Uses .create() to claim membership before increment; catch ALREADY_EXISTS skips the increment. Safe.
Uses eventRef.create() which atomically fails on duplicate — no TOCTOU on first delivery. Only 'failed' retry-window has residual race (F5-005).
Pure FieldValue.increment with no read-modify-write. Atomic. Safe.
